---
title: "My Website"
---

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%" style="text-align: center;"}

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align='center', out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/image.jpg")
```

## Emily Wilhelm

:::

::: {.column width="50%" style="text-align: left;"}


I recently earned my MPH in Environmental Health from Emory Rollins School of Public Health, with certificates in Data Science and Climate & Health. My research at Rollins focuses on using statistical and geospatial analysis to evaluate how environmental exposures, such as air pollution and heat, impact health. I integrate complex datasets to identify exposure patterns, assess risk, and support data-driven public health decision-making, and I have experience translating findings for both technical and community audiences. 

:::
